name: Flor-a-Mar
short_description: A low-elevation Florida rockhounding site near Elfers offering opportunities to find quartz, agate, and chalcedony specimens. This coastal plain location provides accessible collecting in Pasco Cou...
description:
Flor-a-Mar is a rockhounding location in Pasco County, Florida, situated in the flat coastal plain at just 3 feet elevation near Elfers. This area lies within Florida's phosphate deposit region, where Eocene-era sediments and phosphatic beds have created conditions favorable for finding quartz nodules, agate fragments, and chalcedony specimens. The completely flat terrain and minimal elevation change make this an accessible destination for rockhounders of all experience levels.

The geology here reflects Florida's unique position as a former shallow marine environment. During the Eocene epoch, silica-rich groundwater percolated through phosphatic sediments, creating nodular quartz deposits and agate-filled pockets in the surrounding clay and sand matrices. These minerals are often found weathered from their original host rock, scattered across the surface or exposed in small quarry cuts and drainage areas throughout Pasco County.

Visitors to Flor-a-Mar should expect a warm, humid climate typical of central Florida's Gulf coastal plain. The flat terrain means easy walking and minimal scrambling, though the vegetation can be thick and the ground sometimes muddy, especially after rainfall. This location is best visited during the cooler months when Florida's heat and humidity are more manageable and the threat of afternoon thunderstorms is reduced.

The site offers a good introduction to Florida rockhounding without requiring extensive hiking or special equipment. Specimens found here are typically smaller quartz pieces, agate nodules, and chalcedony fragments suitable for displays, tumbling, or jewelry projects. The accessibility of the area makes it an ideal location for families and beginners interested in learning about Florida's geological resources.
history: This region sits within Florida's Eocene phosphate belt, where ancient marine environments 50 million years ago deposited thick sequences of phosphatic sediments and silica-rich beds. These deposits, weathered and exposed through groundwater action and natural erosion over millennia, have created the quartz and agate specimens found at Flor-a-Mar today.
tips: Search around small drainage areas, ditches, and disturbed ground where quartz nodules and agate fragments are more easily exposed by water action and erosion. Look for translucent to milky white quartz pieces and banded agate in shades of white, gray, and occasionally tan or brown. Use a small shovel or trowel to gently dig into the upper soil layers and sift through sandy areas where weathered specimens accumulate. Bring plenty of water and insect repellent, as the warm climate and vegetation attract mosquitoes and other insects.
terrain_notes: Completely flat terrain at 3 feet elevation with minimal slope. The ground is typically sandy to clayey with mixed vegetation including palmettos, pine, and scrubby plants common to central Florida's coastal plain. Walking distances from parking are usually short, but the vegetation can be dense. Footing is generally easy on flat ground, though wet or muddy conditions can occur after rain.
best_season: October - April. Florida's summer heat and humidity (May - September) are oppressive, and afternoon thunderstorms are frequent. The cooler, drier winter months provide the most comfortable rockhounding conditions.
written_directions: From Elfers, head south on US 19 for approximately 2 miles. Turn east toward the Flor-a-Mar area near the coordinates 28.233789, -82.747869. Local roads and property access roads provide approach to collecting areas. Be aware that some areas may be on private property or require permission. Confirm access before arriving.
road_conditions: Local roads are paved or well-maintained dirt roads typical of Pasco County. Standard passenger vehicle access is possible.
parking_notes: Parking availability depends on specific private property access. Roadside pullouts may be available in some areas. Always confirm parking and access with property owners.
collection_rules: This is private property. You must obtain permission from the landowner before collecting. Respect all posted property signs and boundaries. Collection is strictly personal use only - no commercial sale or trade of specimens. Remove only what you can carry and what you intend to keep. Leave the area in good condition and do not disturb the surrounding environment.
rules:
Obtain landowner permission before collecting
Personal use only - no commercial collection
Respect property boundaries and posted signs
Leave area undisturbed and in good condition
nearest_services: Elfers, approximately 2 miles north. Offers basic services including small convenience stores and gas stations. For more comprehensive services (restaurants, larger shops, medical facilities), Dade City is approximately 10 miles north.
accessibility_notes: Relatively flat terrain accessible to most hikers and visitors with varying mobility levels. Completely level ground with minimal elevation change. Vegetation can be dense, potentially limiting wheelchair access in some areas. Sturdy footwear recommended due to possible sandy or muddy conditions. The flat terrain and short walking distances make this suitable for families and those with limited mobility.

faq.question: What gemstones can you find rockhounding near Elfers at Flor-a-Mar?
faq.answer: Flor-a-Mar offers opportunities to find quartz, agate, and chalcedony specimens. These minerals occur naturally in Pasco County's phosphate-rich geology and can be found as weathered nodules and fragments scattered across the surface or exposed in sandy areas. Most specimens are small to medium-sized quartz pieces and agate fragments suitable for collections or tumbling.

faq.question: Is Flor-a-Mar a free rockhounding site or do you need to pay?
faq.answer: Flor-a-Mar is private property, so there is no fee. However, you must obtain permission from the landowner before collecting any specimens. Always confirm access rights before visiting, as collection without permission is trespassing. Contact local property owners or check with Pasco County for current access information.

faq.question: What is the best time of year to go rockhounding at Flor-a-Mar in Florida?
faq.answer: October through April is the ideal rockhounding season at Flor-a-Mar. Florida's summer months (May - September) bring intense heat, high humidity, and frequent afternoon thunderstorms that make outdoor collecting uncomfortable and unsafe. The cooler winter months provide pleasant conditions and lower insect activity.

faq.question: Is Flor-a-Mar rockhounding site suitable for beginners and families?
faq.answer: Yes, Flor-a-Mar is very beginner-friendly and family-suitable. The completely flat terrain at sea level requires no hiking or technical skills, making it accessible to rockhounders of all ages and experience levels. The short walking distances and easy ground conditions make it an ideal introduction to Florida rockhounding for families and children age 6 and up.

faq.question: What tools and equipment do you need to collect gemstones at Flor-a-Mar?
faq.answer: Basic rockhounding tools work well at Flor-a-Mar. Bring a small shovel or trowel for gentle digging in sandy soil, a classifier or sieve for sifting, and a backpack or bag for carrying specimens. Most importantly, bring plenty of water and insect repellent due to Florida's warm climate and mosquitoes. Sturdy footwear is recommended for muddy or sandy conditions.

faq.question: How far is Flor-a-Mar rockhounding site from major Florida cities?
faq.answer: Flor-a-Mar is located approximately 2 miles south of Elfers, about 10 miles south of Dade City, roughly 40 miles north of Tampa, and approximately 60 miles southwest of Orlando. The site is accessible via US 19 and local roads in Pasco County. Most Florida rockhounders can reach this central location within 1-2 hours of driving from major population centers.

meta_description: Find quartz, agate, and chalcedony at Flor-a-Mar near Elfers, Florida. Easy private collecting in Pasco County's coastal plain. Plan your trip.
